title: Happy Hour

jokes.text: Not counting the last one, 25. Count the last one. Okay, 25 and one girl pushup. Oh, new record!
jokes.setup: Employee reports doing 25 pushups, establishing a fitness challenge
jokes.punchline: The 'new record' is pathetically low - only 26 pushups total with one being a 'girl pushup'
jokes.explanation: Solid workplace comedy setup with good economy - the absurdly low 'record' is funny

jokes.text: Anyone who does more than 25 pushups... And one girl pushup. ...gets to go home.
jokes.setup: Michael offers incentive for doing more pushups than the 'record'
jokes.punchline: The interruption to specify 'and one girl pushup' shows Michael's petty attention to detail
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Michael's character - he can't let the detail go

jokes.text: I say 'dance,' they say 'how high?'
jokes.setup: Michael believes he has authority over his employees
jokes.punchline: He mixes up the idiom 'jump/how high' with 'dance' - revealing his incompetence
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - perfectly captures Michael's malapropism tendency and delusions of authority

jokes.text: I had a really hard workout this morning. Oh, wow! That is adorable!
jokes.setup: Jim makes excuse for poor pushup performance
jokes.punchline: Pam's patronizing 'adorable' response treats him like a child making excuses
jokes.explanation: Nice couple dynamic - Pam's condescending tone is perfectly delivered

jokes.text: Essentially what we have here is one of those stories where a mother lifts a car to save her baby.
jokes.setup: Stanley unexpectedly performing well at pushups
jokes.punchline: Jim's comparison to superhuman maternal strength for Stanley doing exercise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy that's both specific and absurd - perfectly captures the unlikely nature of Stanley exercising

jokes.text: Well, it's not exactly fair. He's got all this weight that's helping him go down.
jokes.setup: Stanley doing surprisingly well at pushups
jokes.punchline: Dwight suggests Stanley's weight gives him an unfair advantage going down
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - inappropriately logical and insensitive about Stanley's weight

jokes.text: All right, Papa Bear.
jokes.setup: Stanley has just completed an impressive physical feat
jokes.punchline: Jim's affectionate but condescending nickname for Stanley
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Jim's tendency to give people affectionate nicknames

jokes.text: Hey, are you doing anything later tonight? I don't know. I'm free. If you hear of anything going on, let me know.
jokes.setup: Oscar asking Matt about evening plans
jokes.punchline: The awkward indirect way of trying to make plans without actually making plans
jokes.explanation: Realistic awkward social interaction - captures the difficulty of making plans when interested in someone

jokes.text: No. I always come in at 7:00. No you don't. Well...
jokes.setup: Oscar claims he always comes in at 7:00
jokes.punchline: Matt immediately contradicts him, creating awkward moment
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective awkwardness from being called out on a lie

jokes.text: Yeah, we talked this morning, and we talked at Christmas, so a little momentum there.
jokes.setup: Oscar trying to gauge his relationship with Matt
jokes.punchline: Two conversations months apart being characterized as 'momentum'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the desperation of trying to find connection in minimal interactions

jokes.text: Has that ever happened? Ever? Didn't we? I think we did.
jokes.setup: Darryl suggesting happy hour as if it's a regular occurrence
jokes.punchline: Oscar's questioning reveals it's never actually happened
jokes.explanation: Good workplace comedy - the false history of office social events

jokes.text: You can be gay with Matt, just be straight with me.
jokes.setup: Darryl talking to Oscar about his interest in Matt
jokes.punchline: Play on 'straight' meaning both honest and heterosexual
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - economical and memorable

jokes.text: I love going to bars with Bob. I tend to wear something low-cut, get men to flirt with me, and Bob beats them up.
jokes.setup: Phyllis expressing enthusiasm about going to bars
jokes.punchline: Her method involves using herself as bait for Bob to commit violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - perfectly captures Phyllis's manipulative nature and dark relationship dynamic

jokes.text: I gotta tell you, this baby is amazing. She gets me out of everything. And I love her. I also love her very much.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining how the baby affects his social obligations
jokes.punchline: The awkward over-correction to clarify he loves his daughter after admitting she's convenient
jokes.explanation: Perfect new parent comedy - the guilt about finding practical benefits in your child

jokes.text: It's been so long since I've been with adults. I'm so excited to see everybody. Creed, Ryan. Oh, my God, Stanley!
jokes.setup: Pam expressing excitement about adult interaction
jokes.punchline: Her enthusiasm for seeing the most problematic coworkers undermines her 'adult' claim
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Creed and Ryan are hardly examples of mature adult company

jokes.text: Talk to me that way again and I'll cut your face off.
jokes.setup: Someone (presumably Dwight) speaking to Angela in a certain tone
jokes.punchline: Angela's extremely violent threat over a minor communication issue
jokes.explanation: Classic Angela - disproportionate violent response to minor slights

jokes.text: But we're kind of keeping it quiet for now, 'cause it's still kind of a new thing, it's a little delicate, and we just don't want all the drama.
jokes.setup: Andy and Erin explaining their secret relationship
jokes.punchline: They're clearly being filmed confessing their 'secret' to a documentary crew
jokes.explanation: Standard mockumentary irony - keeping secrets while talking to cameras

jokes.text: Well, when a new mom hears a baby cry, her you-know-whats fill up with you know what, and then her shirt gets, you know... That would be funny.
jokes.setup: Kevin trying to explain lactation euphemistically
jokes.punchline: His awkward explanation ending with 'That would be funny' shows his inappropriate perspective
jokes.explanation: Peak Kevin - inappropriate subject matter handled with his characteristic clumsiness

jokes.text: It's not a birthday, it's not a goodbye party.
jokes.setup: People being excited about the happy hour
jokes.punchline: Stanley's deadpan dismissal of their enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley - bringing everyone down to earth with practical perspective

jokes.text: That would be sublime.
jokes.setup: Being asked to be fourth for pool
jokes.punchline: Dwight's overly formal and elevated response to a casual invitation
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight formality applied to mundane social situation

jokes.text: I'll call this a deck if it'll make you happy, but this is just a porch without a roof.
jokes.setup: Darryl describing someone's deck construction
jokes.punchline: His practical, no-nonsense assessment deflating someone's pride
jokes.explanation: Good Darryl character moment - practical expertise delivered bluntly

jokes.text: Oh, no, no. Pam and Jim work for me, and if they win, they are fired. Not really. Not really. But they better not win.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his relationship with Pam and Jim to Julie
jokes.punchline: The progression from joke threat to serious clarification back to actual threat
jokes.explanation: Good Michael characterization - can't maintain the joke facade when his competitiveness surfaces

jokes.text: See, I didn't think you could teach that. I thought that was something you were born with. What am I thinking right now? Are you thinking that I said ESP? Yes. I feel like an idiot.
jokes.setup: Michael learning Isabel teaches ESL (English as Second Language)
jokes.punchline: Michael confuses ESL with ESP (extrasensory perception) and tests her psychic abilities
jokes.explanation: Excellent Michael moment - perfectly captures his tendency toward malapropisms and doubling down on mistakes

jokes.text: I was a little nervous when Pam told me he was her boss, but he doesn't act like a boss at all. If I had a boss like that, we'd never get anything done.
jokes.setup: Isabel giving her impression of Michael
jokes.punchline: Her 'compliment' accidentally identifies the exact problem with Michael as a manager
jokes.explanation: Great outsider perspective joke - she unknowingly identifies the core problem with Michael's management

jokes.text: Well, apparently Michael Scott is on a date, and that... That, my friend, changes everything.
jokes.setup: Dwight observing Michael with Isabel
jokes.punchline: Dwight treating Michael's dating like a momentous strategic development
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - treating normal social situations like military intelligence

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Date Mike. Nice to meet me. How do you like your eggs in the morning?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to be suave when Jim interrupts his date
jokes.punchline: The cringey 'Date Mike' persona with the inappropriate breakfast question
jokes.explanation: Iconic Michael moment - 'Date Mike' became a cultural reference point for cringey dating behavior

jokes.text: Do you want to just make a run for it? Maybe.
jokes.setup: Isabel experiencing Michael's Date Mike persona
jokes.punchline: Immediately wanting to escape after meeting 'Date Mike'
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction to Date Mike - economical and realistic response to cringe behavior

jokes.text: Wow! Can you imagine what people would say if they saw us dancing together? Oh, I know. They'd be like, 'What's up with those two?' 'Hey, guys, get a bedroom already.' 'Did we miss the wedding?'
jokes.setup: Andy and Erin imagining public reaction to them dancing
jokes.punchline: Their elaborate fantasy of people caring about their relationship when they're being deliberately secretive
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - their self-importance about a relationship no one cares about

jokes.text: Angela versus Isabel. Height, advantage Isabel. Birthing hips, advantage Isabel. Remaining childbearing years, advantage Isabel. Legal obligation, advantage Angela.
jokes.setup: Dwight comparing Angela and Isabel
jokes.punchline: His clinical breeding assessment ending with the contract obligation
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight - treating women like livestock while acknowledging his contractual obligations. Perfectly structured escalation.

jokes.text: I would say I kind of have an unfair advantage, because I watch reality dating shows like a hawk. And I learn. I absorb information from the strategies of the winners and the losers. Actually, I probably learn more from the losers.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his dating expertise
jokes.punchline: Learning from reality TV losers while being completely clueless about actual dating
jokes.explanation: Great Michael delusion - thinking reality TV makes him a dating expert while failing spectacularly

jokes.text: No, no, no, no, no. I want a big family. Tall, thick. A big, physically big family.
jokes.setup: Dwight breaking up with Angela about wanting different things
jokes.punchline: His clarification that he means physically large people, not numerous children
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight moment - his literal interpretation of 'big family' reveals his breeding obsession

jokes.text: You want to pay 400 bucks to re-felt this table? Yeah! Why don't you send the bill to 23 I Don't Care Lane, Scranton, Pennsylvania?
jokes.setup: Bar manager asking about damage to pool table
jokes.punchline: Michael's elaborate fake address to avoid responsibility
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael escalation - elaborate scheme to avoid a simple problem

jokes.text: Maybe it should come from a note. With flowers. Tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Discussing how to explain Michael's behavior to Julie
jokes.punchline: Jim's suggestion to increasingly distance themselves from the conversation
jokes.explanation: Good Jim response showing his desire to avoid awkward conversations

jokes.text: We got a problem? Yes. Homelessness.
jokes.setup: Confrontational question about behavior in the bar
jokes.punchline: Michael deflecting to a completely unrelated social issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - unexpected and absurd response that's perfectly Michael

jokes.text: Well, it just so happens that I'm a manager, too, and the way I manage people is that I touch their hearts and souls with humor, with love and maybe a dash of razzle-dazzle, and I don't see that from you.
jokes.setup: Michael arguing with the bar manager
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous management philosophy delivered during a bar confrontation
jokes.explanation: Great Michael delusion - applying his terrible management theory to a bar dispute

jokes.text: I've written all of it. In my head.
jokes.setup: Bar manager asking about Michael's management book
jokes.punchline: Michael's book exists only in his imagination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - grandiose claims undermined by reality

jokes.text: Read it? I own it. But no, I have not read it.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's read Lee Iacocca's book
jokes.punchline: Michael's contradiction revealing he owns books but doesn't read them
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael contradiction - trying to seem intellectual while admitting he doesn't read

jokes.text: I don't normally do this, but... Do what? Go, go. Just sit down next to a beautiful woman and start talking to myself to confuse other people.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to create a cover story for their relationship
jokes.punchline: His explanation makes their behavior sound even more bizarre
jokes.explanation: Good Andy awkwardness - his 'cover' story is worse than the truth

jokes.text: Hey, big boy. Do you like it when I do that? What are you doing? What we said to do. We didn't say we were gonna, like, start groping strangers.
jokes.setup: Erin following Andy's plan to throw people off their scent
jokes.punchline: Erin completely misunderstanding and sexually harassing a stranger
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Erin's innocent misinterpretation leads to inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: What movie? Black Snake Moan?
jokes.setup: Erin says she learned to flirt from movies
jokes.punchline: Andy's reference to a particularly inappropriate film for flirting lessons
jokes.explanation: Good specific reference that shows Andy's media knowledge and slight inappropriateness

jokes.text: Stanley Hudson? No. No. Whoa! A lot of Stanley Hudsons in here. No, it's Michael Scott.
jokes.setup: Bar manager finding Michael's business card in a bowl
jokes.punchline: Michael's ridiculous excuse about multiple Stanley Hudsons
jokes.explanation: Good Michael deflection - absurd explanation that makes no sense but he commits to it

jokes.text: Stanley Hudson? No. No. Whoa! A lot of Stanley Hudsons in here. No, it's Michael Scott.
jokes.setup: Bartender reads business card name incorrectly
jokes.punchline: Michael's explanation that there are multiple Stanley Hudsons present
jokes.explanation: Michael's inability to admit mistake, creates absurd explanation instead

jokes.text: He's more just... Like... You can go. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Julie asks what Michael is usually like
jokes.punchline: Pam struggles to describe him positively, gives up and lets Julie leave
jokes.explanation: Good beat showing Pam's inability to defend Michael's behavior

jokes.text: I saw you talking to Hide. Did you hear that dude's life story? It's amazing, right? I couldn't understand a word he said.
jokes.setup: Darryl asking Oscar about Hide's story
jokes.punchline: Claiming it's amazing while admitting he understood nothing
jokes.explanation: Good social awkwardness - pretending to connect while admitting failure to understand

jokes.text: Matt's an okay dude, but he's a dummy. You guys got nothing in common.
jokes.setup: Darryl giving Oscar dating advice about Matt
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment that undermines Oscar's romantic hopes
jokes.explanation: Classic Darryl bluntness - practical advice delivered without sugar-coating

jokes.text: My name is Andrew Bernard and I've been on two dates with Erin Hannon and they went well, and there will probably be more. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Andy deciding to announce his relationship publicly
jokes.punchline: The formal, awkward announcement treating dating like a business presentation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy - treating normal social situations like formal presentations

jokes.text: Dwight recently entered into a contract with me, establishing intent to conceive and raise a child with me. Did he not tell you that?
jokes.setup: Angela confronting Dwight about their contract
jokes.punchline: Treating reproduction like a legal business arrangement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela/Dwight dynamic - reducing romance to contractual obligations

jokes.text: Nice to meet me.
jokes.setup: Michael ending the night
jokes.punchline: Bringing back the Date Mike catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Date Mike - economical way to bookend the episode

tmdb_overview: When the sales staff lets Sabre's new "sales is king" policy get to their heads, Michael hides their new, prized leads.
